name: Potato Skins with Salsa and Cheese
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Diced, seeded, firm, ripe tomatoes
1/4 cup Rinsed, drained canned black beans
1 tablespoon Finely chopped green bell pepper
1 teaspoon Seeded, finely chopped jalapeno or other pepper
2 tablespoons Shredded part-time mozzarella or reduced-fat Monterey Jack cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Wash the potatoes thoroughly; pierce with the tip of a knife and bake directly on the oven rack until tender, about 45 minutes. Let cool.
3. Meanwhile, make the salsa: In a bowl, combine the diced tomatoes, corn, black beans, cucumber, scallion, 2 tablespoons olive oil, green bell pepper, cilantro, lime juice, and jalapeno; add salt to taste.
4. Halve the potatoes lengthwise and carefully remove all but 1/8"-1/4" of the potato, leaving the skin intact. Reserve the insides of the potatoes for another use.
5. Brush the inside of the potato shells lightly with the tablespoon olive oil.
6. Preheat the broiler.
7. Place the potato skins cut side up on a baking sheet and broil 3-4 inches from the flame for 5 minutes. Turn the skins over and broil on the other side for 1 minute.
8. Spoon the salsa into the potato skins, dividing it evenly. Sprinkle with the cheese, dividing it evenly.
9. Broil the skins just until the cheese is melted. Serve immediately.
Nutritional analysis per serving: 180 calories; 9 grams total fat; 4 grams protein; 23 grams carbohydrates; 2 milligrams cholesterol; 105 milligrams sodium.
